title: Getting started
text: trisk.analysis measures climate transition risk level individual companies portfolios built — wherever transition risk placed book exposures, whether banks, central banks supervisors, asset managers. underlying TRISK engine (trisk.model) asset-level transition-risk stress test: feeds climate scenarios microeconomic model firm uses company- asset-level data value scenario-driven declines high-carbon production stranded-asset exposure adjust company’s value relative business--usual baseline. TRISK turns scenario assumptions shocked net present values (NPVs) probabilities default (PDs). package wraps engine can run directly portfolio, allocate shocks onto exposures — loans equity holdings — blend internal PD/EL estimates. worked vignettes follow bank credit-risk workflow; institution types can adapt steps.

title: Getting started
text: worked vignettes follow bank credit-risk workflow. Work order: vignette("bank_1_portfolio-analysis") — bank entry point: five inputs, setting trisk_inputs/ data folder, first end--end run. vignette("bank_2_inputs--outputs") — TRISK consumes (assets, scenarios, financial features, carbon prices) produces (NPV PD results). vignette("bank_3_run---portfolio") — run TRISK loan book map shocks onto exposures. vignette("bank_4_sensitivity-analysis") — sweep scenario assumptions see results move across baselines shock scenarios. vignette("bank_5_pd-el-integration") — blend TRISK shocks PD/EL get climate-adjusted expected loss.

title: Getting started
text: smallest end--end run: load four bundled trisk.model inputs bundled portfolio, call run_trisk_on_simple_portfolio(). input row comes back climate-shocked exposure loss expected loss alongside original exposure.
code: library(trisk.analysis) assets <- read.csv(system.file("testdata", "assets_testdata.csv", package = "trisk.model", mustWork = TRUE)) scenarios <- read.csv(system.file("testdata", "scenarios_testdata.csv", package = "trisk.model", mustWork = TRUE)) financial_features <- read.csv(system.file("testdata", "financial_features_testdata.csv", package = "trisk.model", mustWork = TRUE)) ngfs_carbon_price <- read.csv(system.file("testdata", "ngfs_carbon_price_testdata.csv", package = "trisk.model", mustWork = TRUE))  portfolio <- read.csv(   system.file("testdata", "portfolio_ids_internal_pd_testdata.csv", package = "trisk.analysis", mustWork = TRUE) )  results <- run_trisk_on_simple_portfolio(   assets_data = assets,   scenarios_data = scenarios,   financial_data = financial_features,   carbon_data = ngfs_carbon_price,   portfolio_data = portfolio,   baseline_scenario = "NGFS2023GCAM_CP",   target_scenario = "NGFS2023GCAM_NZ2050",   scenario_geography = "Global" ) #> -- Start Trisk-- Retyping Dataframes.  #> -- Processing Assets and Scenarios.  #> -- Transforming to Trisk model input.  #> -- Calculating baseline, target, and shock trajectories.  #> -- Applying zero-trajectory logic to production trajectories.  #> -- Calculating net profits. #> Joining with `by = join_by(asset_id, company_id, sector, technology)` #> -- Calculating market risk.  #> -- Calculating credit risk.  head(results$portfolio_results) #> # title: Getting started
text: run_trisk_on_simple_portfolio() forwards extra arguments TRISK engine (trisk.model::run_trisk_model()). scenarios required; every argument default, shown meaning. Set match cost--capital assumptions prevailing market curve analysis date. See vignette("bank_2_inputs--outputs") fuller definitions parameter.
code: results <- run_trisk_on_simple_portfolio(   assets_data        = assets,   scenarios_data     = scenarios,   financial_data     = financial_features,   carbon_data        = ngfs_carbon_price,   portfolio_data     = portfolio,   baseline_scenario  = "NGFS2023GCAM_CP",       # business-as-usual scenario   target_scenario    = "NGFS2023GCAM_NZ2050",   # shock (policy) scenario   scenario_geography = "Global",                # region(s) to compute results for   carbon_price_model = "no_carbon_tax",         # NGFS carbon-price pathway ("no_carbon_tax" to skip)   risk_free_rate     = 0.045,                   # risk-free rate (Merton PD model)   discount_rate      = 0.09,                    # DCF discount rate on dividends   growth_rate        = 0.03,                    # terminal growth rate of profits   div_netprofit_prop_coef = 1,                  # dividend pass-through coefficient   shock_year         = 2030,                    # year the policy shock is applied   market_passthrough = 0                        # firm's ability to pass carbon cost to consumers )

title: Bank workflow 2: Inputs and outputs
text: test data small fixed. real analysis, download full production inputs (assets, scenarios, financial features carbon prices) public endpoint: download_trisk_inputs() fetches production scenarios.csv folder choice (skipped vignette network dependency). three reference inputs — assets, financial_features ngfs_carbon_price — supply , starting bundled samples (see Bank portfolio analysis setup_trisk_inputs()): downloaded read CSVs way test data. production scenarios.csv covers many scenarios bundled sample: six providers (NGFS, WEO, IPR, Oxford, GECO Mission Possible Partnership steel), one data vintages. Scenario names encode provider, vintage scenario, e.g. WEO2023_STEPS IEA World Energy Outlook 2023 “Stated Policies” baseline; NGFS2023GCAM_NZ2050 NGFS 2023 “Net Zero 2050” target run GCAM integrated-assessment model. rule, pick baseline target provider. bundled scenarios already expose valid baseline/target pairings via get_available_parameters() (run test data needs network): choosing scenario consider: data vintage (newer usually relevant), geography coverage (NGFS GCAM offers sub-global regions), target’s ambition level (e.g. NGFS B2DS 2 degrees vs NZ2050 net zero), narrative — NGFS scenarios “LD” (Low Demand) reach net zero demand assumptions rather policy alone. See https://www.ngfs.net/en narrative detail.

title: Bank workflow 2: Inputs and outputs
text: smallest runnable stress test bundled data. parameters fall two groups: scenario selection (pathways compare ) financial assumptions (DCF PD model knobs). Parameter reference: baseline_scenario, target_scenario: status-quo shock scenario names. scenario_geography: region(s) compute. Choosing region "Global" filters assets region’s countries uses region-specific pathways. shock_year: year transition shock applied. carbon_price_model: carbon tax trajectory apply (no_carbon_tax skip). market_passthrough: share carbon tax firm passes customers. risk_free_rate, discount_rate, growth_rate, div_netprofit_prop_coef: DCF PD valuation assumptions. run_trisk_model() runs model returns results asset grain (one row per company / asset / country / technology):
code: baseline_scenario <- "NGFS2023GCAM_CP" target_scenario <- "NGFS2023GCAM_NZ2050" scenario_geography <- "Global" shock_year <- 2030  carbon_price_model <- "no_carbon_tax" risk_free_rate <- 0.045 discount_rate <- 0.09 growth_rate <- 0.03 div_netprofit_prop_coef <- 1 market_passthrough <- 0 st_results <- run_trisk_model(   assets_data = assets_testdata,   scenarios_data = scenarios_testdata,   financial_data = financial_features_testdata,   carbon_data = ngfs_carbon_price_testdata,   baseline_scenario = baseline_scenario,   target_scenario = target_scenario,   scenario_geography = scenario_geography,   shock_year = shock_year,   carbon_price_model = carbon_price_model,   risk_free_rate = risk_free_rate,   discount_rate = discount_rate,   growth_rate = growth_rate,   div_netprofit_prop_coef = div_netprofit_prop_coef,   market_passthrough = market_passthrough ) #> -- Retyping Dataframes.  #> -- Processing Assets and Scenarios.  #> -- Transforming to Trisk model input.  #> -- Calculating baseline, target, and shock trajectories.  #> -- Applying zero-trajectory logic to production trajectories.  #> -- Calculating net profits. #> Joining with `by = join_by(asset_id, company_id, sector, technology)` #> -- Calculating market risk.  #> -- Calculating credit risk.  npv_results <- st_results$npv_results pd_results <- st_results$pd_results company_trajectories <- st_results$company_trajectories

title: Bank workflow 2: Inputs and outputs
text: Since December 2024 TRISK runs company / country / technology grain, improves NPV trajectory accuracy changes output shape relative older analyses. run_trisk_agg() takes arguments aggregates NPVs per company country technology, restoring pre-2024 output structure backward compatibility simpler reporting. PD results unaffected — already company/sector grain functions. aggregated NPV table drops country_iso2 column:
code: st_results_agg <- run_trisk_agg(   assets_data = assets_testdata,   scenarios_data = scenarios_testdata,   financial_data = financial_features_testdata,   carbon_data = ngfs_carbon_price_testdata,   baseline_scenario = baseline_scenario,   target_scenario = target_scenario,   scenario_geography = scenario_geography,   shock_year = shock_year ) #> -- Retyping Dataframes.  #> -- Processing Assets and Scenarios.  #> -- Transforming to Trisk model input.  #> -- Calculating baseline, target, and shock trajectories.  #> -- Applying zero-trajectory logic to production trajectories.  #> -- Calculating net profits. #> Joining with `by = join_by(asset_id, company_id, sector, technology)` #> -- Calculating market risk.  #> -- Calculating credit risk.  npv_results_agg <- st_results_agg$npv_results

title: Bank workflow 2: Inputs and outputs

Coerce once here so every downstream join on # `company_id` matches on type. portfolio_ids_internal_pd$company_id <- as.character(portfolio_ids_internal_pd$company_id)  stopifnot(   "Portfolio file must include an `internal_pd` column per exposure." =     "internal_pd" %in% colnames(portfolio_ids_internal_pd),   "`internal_pd` values must be numeric in [0, 1]." =     is.numeric(portfolio_ids_internal_pd$internal_pd) &&       all(portfolio_ids_internal_pd$internal_pd >= 0 &             portfolio_ids_internal_pd$internal_pd <= 1, na.rm = TRUE) )  portfolio_terms <- portfolio_ids_internal_pd[, c("company_id", "term", "exposure_value_usd")]

text: three functions defined inline vignette (exported package). one takes long-format PD output run_trisk_sa(), attaches bank’s contractual portfolio terms, produces comparison plot PD difference (pd_shock - pd_baseline) per variant. small palette shared across three dimension sections variant index gets colour everywhere:
code: attach_portfolio_term <- function(sa_pd, portfolio_terms) {   joined <- sa_pd %>%     dplyr::inner_join(portfolio_terms, by = c("company_id", "term"))   present <- dplyr::distinct(joined[, c("company_id", "term")])   n_dropped <- nrow(portfolio_terms) - nrow(present)   if (n_dropped > 0) {     dropped <- dplyr::anti_join(portfolio_terms, present, by = c("company_id", "term"))     warning("Dropped ", n_dropped, " portfolio row(s) whose term is outside the Merton grid: ",             paste(dropped$company_id, dropped$term, sep = "/term=", collapse = ", "),             call. = FALSE)   }   joined }  label_by_variant <- function(sa_result, label_fn) {   sa_result$pd %>%     dplyr::left_join(sa_result$params, by = "run_id") %>%     dplyr::mutate(variant = label_fn(.)) }  draw_pd_by_sector <- function(labelled_pd, portfolio_terms, variant_name,                               palette_values = NULL) {   agg <- labelled_pd %>%     attach_portfolio_term(portfolio_terms) %>%     dplyr::mutate(pd_difference = .data$pd_shock - .data$pd_baseline) %>%     dplyr::group_by(.data$sector, .data$variant) %>%     dplyr::summarise(       pd_difference = stats::weighted.mean(.data$pd_difference,                                            w = .data$exposure_value_usd,                                            na.rm = TRUE),       .groups = "drop"     )   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(agg, ggplot2::aes(x = .data$variant,                                          y = .data$pd_difference,                                          fill = .data$variant)) +     ggplot2::geom_col(width = 0.7) +     ggplot2::facet_grid(. ~ sector, scales = "free_y") +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(       trans  = scales::pseudo_log_trans(sigma = 1e-7),       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.0001)     ) +     TRISK_PLOT_THEME_FUNC() +     ggplot2::labs(x = variant_name, y = "EAD-weighted PD difference (shock - baseline)",                   fill = variant_name,                   title = paste("PD sensitivity to", variant_name, "by sector"),                   subtitle = "Actual portfolio terms; EAD-weighted; pseudo-log y per sector")   if (!is.null(palette_values)) p <- p + ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = palette_values)   p }  draw_pd_by_exposure <- function(labelled_pd, portfolio_terms, variant_name,                                 palette_values = NULL) {   agg <- labelled_pd %>%     attach_portfolio_term(portfolio_terms) %>%     dplyr::mutate(firm = paste(.data$company_id, .data$sector, sep = "/"),                   pd_difference = .data$pd_shock - .data$pd_baseline)   p <- ggplot2::ggplot(agg, ggplot2::aes(x = .data$firm,                                          y = .data$pd_difference,                                          fill = .data$variant)) +     ggplot2::geom_col(position = ggplot2::position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.7) +     ggplot2::facet_grid(. ~ sector, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +     ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(       trans  = scales::pseudo_log_trans(sigma = 1e-7),       labels = scales::percent_format(accuracy = 0.0001)     ) +     TRISK_PLOT_THEME_FUNC() +     ggplot2::theme(axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +     ggplot2::labs(x = "Firm / sector", y = "PD difference (shock - baseline)",                   fill = variant_name,                   title = paste("Per-exposure PD sensitivity to", variant_name),                   subtitle = "Actual portfolio terms; pseudo-log y per sector")   if (!is.null(palette_values)) p <- p + ggplot2::scale_fill_manual(values = palette_values)   p } TRAJ_PALETTE <- c("#1b324f", "#00c082", "#ff9623")  # matches plot_multi_trajectories()

title: Bank workflow 5: PD & EL integration
text: Let p_int internal PD, p_base TRISK baseline PD, p_shock TRISK shock PD given exposure. method returns adjusted PD p_adj, integrate_pd() clips [0, 1]. Absolute — add raw PD shift onto internal PD: padj=pint+(pshock−pbase) p_{adj} = p_{int} + (p_{shock} - p_{base}) Relative — scale internal PD proportional shift: padj=pint(1+pshock−pbasepbase),(pbase≠0) p_{adj} = p_{int}\left(1 + \frac{p_{shock} - p_{base}}{p_{base}}\right),    \qquad (p_{base} \neq 0) p_base = 0 relative method returns p_int unchanged — shock signal lost zero-baseline rows. Prefer absolute zscore . Z-score (default) — recombine three PDs normal-quantile (probit) space. Φ\Phi standard-normal CDF Φ−1\Phi^{-1} quantile function, clipping PD [zscore_floor, zscore_cap] quantile: padj=Φ(Φ−1(pint)+Φ−1(pshock)−Φ−1(pbase)) p_{adj} = \Phi\!\Big(\Phi^{-1}(p_{int}) + \Phi^{-1}(p_{shock})    - \Phi^{-1}(p_{base})\Big) probit / Merton-style recombination default thresholds, Basel IRB risk-weight formula. adds TRISK shock-minus-baseline distance--default shift internal PD’s implied default threshold, maps back probability. right default zero-safe (via clipping), preserves non-linear compression PDs near distribution tails, behaves sensibly sparse portfolios Merton inputs drive baseline PDs underflow. invoke Basel asset-correlation / Vasicek conditional-PD capital formula — correlation parameter supervisory confidence level . zscore_floor (default 1e-4) zscore_cap (default 1 - 1e-4) parameters bound PDs qnorm(), since qnorm(0) = -Inf qnorm(1) = +Inf otherwise propagate infinities. Widen floor toward zero internal PDs reliably calibrated low; tighten de-sensitise tails.

what: Worked example: one row through z-score
title: Bank workflow 5: PD & EL integration
text: Take single Oil&Gas exposure internal_pd = 0.025, suppose TRISK returns pd_baseline = 0.020 pd_shock = 0.060 . None three values hit clip bounds, : probit shift z_shk - z_base positive (shock raises model PD), recombined internal PD rises 0.025 — worsening, plots render red.
code: z_int  <- qnorm(0.025)   # internal z_base <- qnorm(0.020)   # TRISK baseline z_shk  <- qnorm(0.060)   # TRISK shock p_adj  <- pnorm(z_int + z_shk - z_base) round(c(z_internal = z_int, z_baseline = z_base, z_shock = z_shk,         adjusted_pd = p_adj, adjustment_pp = (p_adj - 0.025) * 100), 4) #>    z_internal    z_baseline       z_shock   adjusted_pd adjustment_pp  #>       -1.9600       -2.0537       -1.5548        0.0720        4.7009

what: EL sign convention, LGD, EAD, horizon
title: Bank workflow 5: PD & EL integration
text: EL sign convention. expected_loss_* columns three integrate_el() methods store EL positive magnitude. Direction lives adjustment, level: positive EL adjustment means expected loss = worse = rendered red; negative adjustment means less loss = better = green. PD adjustment follows rule (positive = PD rose = red). EAD. exposure_at_default column raw exposure. compute_analysis_metrics() defines exposure_value_usd * loss_given_default — .e. LGD already folded . Every expected_loss_* column exposure_at_default * pd. EL zscore method uses exposure_at_default column denominator present, otherwise reconstructs exposure_value_usd * loss_given_default. LGD. loss_given_default fraction lost default, [0, 1]. already baked exposure_at_default, expected loss EL = exposure_at_default * PD (equivalently exposure_value_usd * LGD * PD). EL zscore method back-transforms EL effective PD via |EL| / exposure_at_default, recombines probit space, re-scales — LGD enters denominator. Horizon. TRISK PDs term-structured loan term (multi-year), 12-month point--time PD. Treat integrated PD/EL lifetime / multi-year quantity, closer spirit IFRS-9 Stage 2/3 lifetime ECL input 12-month regulatory PD. feed directly 12-month-PD slot without re-annualising.

Bank portfolio analysis — start — entry point bank: five inputs, set trisk_inputs/ data folder setup_trisk_inputs(), central portfolio_ids file (names / countries options), first end--end run. Inputs outputs — 4 input datasets (assets, scenarios, financial_features, ngfs_carbon_prices) 3 output datasets (npv_results, pd_results, company_trajectories), column structure, Trisk parameter definitions, access bundled scenarios. Run portfolio — match portfolio TRISK, compute PD / NPV / expected loss, visualise results. Sensitivity analysis — sweep shock year, IAM, ambition policy, read bank-impact lens. PD & EL integration — recombine TRISK shock PDs internal PDs translate result expected-loss basis points. Note Application code requires availability custom data assets financial data. inputs need pre-processed independantly, following structure mock datasets presented vignettes.

what: Integrate TRISK EL shift into an internal EL estimate — integrate_el
title: Integrate TRISK EL shift into an internal EL estimate — integrate_el
text: Applies one three methods translate TRISK baseline--shock EL change bank's internal EL scale. "absolute" "relative" mirror Shiny EL integration logic; "zscore" adds Basel IRB-aligned Vasicek recombination transforming EL effective PD (|EL| / (EAD * LGD)), applying z-score combination normal-quantile space, converting back. three methods return EL positive magnitude (post 59571f3 package-wide convention). zscore method requires `exposure_value_usd` `loss_given_default` columns `analysis_data`.

title: Integrate TRISK EL shift into an internal EL estimate — integrate_el
code: integrate_el(   analysis_data,   internal_el = NULL,   method = c("zscore", "absolute", "relative"),   zscore_floor = ZSCORE_FLOOR_DEFAULT,   zscore_cap = ZSCORE_CAP_DEFAULT )

title: Integrate TRISK EL shift into an internal EL estimate — integrate_el
text: analysis_data Data frame [run_trisk_on_portfolio()]; must contain columns `expected_loss_baseline`, `expected_loss_shock`. zscore method additionally needs EAD denominator: uses `exposure_at_default` column present (canonical contract, written [compute_analysis_metrics()] [run_trisk_on_simple_portfolio()]) otherwise reconstructs `exposure_value_usd * loss_given_default`. internal_el Numeric vector length `nrow(analysis_data)`, data frame `company_id` + `internal_el` columns, NULL (default) uses `expected_loss_baseline`. method One "zscore", "absolute", "relative". Default "zscore". zscore_floor Lower clip bound effective PDs `qnorm()`. Default 1e-4. zscore_cap Upper clip bound. Default 1 - 1e-4.

title: Integrate TRISK EL shift into an internal EL estimate — integrate_el
text: List `$portfolio`, `$portfolio_long`, `$aggregate`.

title: Integrate TRISK PD shift into an internal PD estimate — integrate_pd
text: analysis_data Data frame [run_trisk_on_portfolio()]; must contain columns `pd_baseline`, `pd_shock`. internal_pd Either () numeric vector length `nrow(analysis_data)`, (b) data frame `company_id` `internal_pd` columns, (c) NULL (default) case `pd_baseline` used. method One "zscore", "absolute", "relative". Default "zscore" (Merton-style probit recombination default thresholds, Basel IRB/Vasicek formula; zero-safe via clipping; recommended sparse portfolios baseline PDs can underflow Merton inputs). zscore_floor Lower clip bound PDs `qnorm()` zscore method. Default 1e-4. zscore_cap Upper clip bound. Default 1 - 1e-4.
